p ***************************************************************************** THIS DOCUMENT IS OUT OF DATE AND IS HERE FOR HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY There is a MATHEMATICA site license, but it is not installed on any EECS Instructional com

is not installed on any EECS Instructional computers. See https://software.berkeley.edu/mathematica. ***************************************************************************** Oct 14, 2015 CONTENTS Overview MATHEMATICA on EECS

4, 2015 CONTENTS Overview MATHEMATICA on EECS Instructional Systems MATHEMATICA Fonts: Downloading to your PC MATHEMATICA Documentation MATHEMATICA Installation on SWW MATHEMATICA Licenses for all UCB Users MATHEMATICA open-source alternativ

all UCB Users MATHEMATICA open-source alternatives Overview -------- Mathematica is an interactive system from Wolfram (http://www.wolfram.com) for doing mathematical computation. It handles numeric, symbolic, and graphical calculations a

numeric, symbolic, and graphical calculations and incorporates a high-level programming language. Mathematica produces graphics in PostScript form. Mathematica and licenses are available from http://software-central.berkeley.edu/. MATHE

m http://software-central.berkeley.edu/. MATHEMATICA on EECS Instructional Systems ----------------------------------------- Instructional users can login to our Solaris SPARC servers such as pulsar.eecs.berkeley.edu quasar.eecs.berk

pulsar.eecs.berkeley.edu quasar.eecs.berkeley.edu to run it (see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clients for a complete list). Here are the UNIX commands that will run Mathematica. The first time you use it: % touch ~/.Xdefaults

first time you use it: % touch ~/.Xdefaults % echo 'XMathematica*usePrivateColormap: True' >> ~/.Xdefaults Then start the program: % set path = ($path /usr/sww/X11/bin); rehash % xset fp+ /usr/sww/pkg/mathematica/SystemFiles/

% xset fp+ /usr/sww/pkg/mathematica/SystemFiles/Fonts/Type1 % /usr/sww/bin/mathematica Fonts: Note that the font directory specified in the "xset" command is local to the computer you are sitting at (where your X Windows manager is runn

e sitting at (where your X Windows manager is running). If you used 'ssh' or 'putty' to login to a remote computer and started mathematica there, you may have to copy the fonts to a directory on your local computer and refer to that directory

your local computer and refer to that directory in the 'xset' command. MATHEMATICA Fonts: Downloading to your PC ----------------------------------------- You can login to our UNIX servers from other computers and run UNIX XWindows program

om other computers and run UNIX XWindows programs (like mathematica) from other computers. Instructions for that are in http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#xwindows. However, if you are using a Microsoft Windows, MacOSX or Linux sys

are using a Microsoft Windows, MacOSX or Linux system, you may find that you lack the specific fonts that Mathematica requires. You can download fonts from: http://support.wolfram.com/mathematica/systems/unix/general/latestfonts.html http:/

ica/systems/unix/general/latestfonts.html http://support.wolfram.com/mathematica/systems/unix/interface/exceed.html Here is how you can install the fonts on a Windows PC so that you can run Mathematica from UNIX. The Windows PC needs an X Wi

hematica from UNIX. The Windows PC needs an X Window manager. The 2 most common ones are Xming and Cygwin X. Cygwin X (http://x.cygwin.com/) ------------------------------- To add fonts to the Cygwin X window manager: 1) Create a folder

the Cygwin X window manager: 1) Create a folder like 'Mathematica' under /usr/lib/X11/fonts/ 2) Copy the BDF/ Type1/ fonts to /usr/lib/X11/fonts/Mathematica/ 3) Startup X ('xinit') and type this in the local xterm window to update the fo

s in the local xterm window to update the font database: xset fp+ /usr/lib/X11/fonts/Mathematica/Type1 xset fp+ /usr/lib/X11/fonts/Mathematica/BDF xset fp rehash 4) Type this in the xterm window to login to the UNIX

ype this in the xterm window to login to the UNIX server (quasar, for example) with X11 forwarding enabled: ssh -X {your-UNIX-account}@quasar.eecs.berkeley.edu 5) On the UNIX server, type "mathematica" or "mathematica6". Xming (/share/b/

athematica" or "mathematica6". Xming (/share/b/pub/xming.help) -------------------------------- To add fonts to the Xming window manager: coming soon... See http://www.straightrunning.com/XmingNotes/fonts.php http://reference.wolfram.co

/XmingNotes/fonts.php http://reference.wolfram.com/mathematica/guide/FontOptions.html Exceed (/share/b/pub/exceed.help) -------------------------------- Requires in addition for fonts to be 'compiled' after copying over. Added problems, t

compiled' after copying over. Added problems, that sometimes there are too many fonts and exceed fails to startup with a message about bdf.fdb being too large to load. Also during the compile phase some new files are generated and what ap

phase some new files are generated and what appears like a database file gets copied to the "All Users" profile area. 1) Copy the BDF font to C:\Program Files\Hummingbird\Connectivity\11.00\Exceed\Font\Mathematica\BDF 2) Then invok

11.00\Exceed\Font\Mathematica\BDF 2) Then invoke Exceed's Xconfig in "Exceed Tools", select "Font" and press the "Edit" button, which will bring up the "Font Database: lfp.xdb" dialog. This displays all the installed fonts, and the fol

This displays all the installed fonts, and the folders they live in. Add the BDF fonts using the "Add Font Directory" button. 3) Hit the "OK" button, which automatically compiles the fonts. 4) When you run exceed now you get the message

4) When you run exceed now you get the message about bdf.fdb being too large to load. It shows up as 65KB. You can delete the mathematica2 fonts (except mathematica2mono*) from the folder as they seem the largest, and don't see

lder as they seem the largest, and don't seem to affect Mathematica running. Without those fonts the file is 61KB. Mathematica has not been exhaustively tested to try the various fonts. MATHEMATICA Documentation ---------------

fonts. MATHEMATICA Documentation ------------------------- Mathematica documentation in Mathematica "notebook" format can be found in: /usr/sww/pkg/mathematica/Documentation /share/b/mathematica/ee121-intro.ma These *.nb files can be rea

tica/ee121-intro.ma These *.nb files can be read using the Help\"The Mathematica Book" menu selection from the Mathematica window. Emacs users may be interested in the "math.el" interface to the Mathematica kernel located in the director

the Mathematica kernel located in the directory /share/b/mathematica on the Instructional UNIX systems. You may also want to browse the man pages for "mathremote", and the Math- ematica World Wide Web site at URL http://www.wolfram.com and

ld Wide Web site at URL http://www.wolfram.com and the Math- ematica news group is comp.soft-sys.math.mathematica. MATHEMATICA Installation on SWW ------------------------------- MATHEMATICA 5.2 is the default using "/usr/sww/bin/mathematica":

2 is the default using "/usr/sww/bin/mathematica": MATHEMATICA 6.0 is available in /usr/sww/pkg/mathematica-6.0/bin/mathematica OS: Solaris SPARC Executables: mathematica graphical user interface to Mathematica
aphical user interface to Mathematica math Mathematica kernel, command-line interface mathremote Use "MathTalk" to communicate between mathematica processs
mathematica processs mcc MathLink template file compiler Library: /usr/sww/mathematica Documentation: /usr/sww/mathematica/man/*, /usr/sww/man Menu selection: He
/usr/sww/man Menu selection: Help\"The Mathematica Book" WWW site http://www.wolfram.com. USENET newsgroup omp.soft-sys.math.mathematica. Also, several copies of the book "Mathematica", b
ies of the book "Mathematica", by Stephen Wolfram, are available for borrowing from several projects in the department. The reference book for the Mathematica kernel is: Stephen Wolfram,

the Mathematica kernel is: Stephen Wolfram, {Mathematica: A System for Doing Mathematics by Computer}, 2nd ed. Redwood City, CA: Addison-Wesley Pub. Co., 1991. Reference books are available in many Instructional labs. The Mathe

ailable in many Instructional labs. The Mathematica notebook interface is described in technical documents included with the Mathematica software. A great tutorial on the Mathematica system is: Nancy Blachman, {Ma

Mathematica system is: Nancy Blachman, {Mathematica, A Practical Approach}. Englewood Cliffs, NJ: Prentice Hall, 1992. Note: Some users, when starting up mathbook, may see warnings about "unknown keysyms". If so, create an en

gs about "unknown keysyms". If so, create an environment variable XKEYSYMDB that points to /usr/sww/X11/lib/X11/XKeysymDB. For example, in csh, % setenv XKEYSYMDB /usr/sww/X11/lib/X11/XKeysymDB MATHEMATICA Licenses for all UCB

11/XKeysymDB MATHEMATICA Licenses for all UCB Users -------------------------------------- Mathematica licenses are available for all UCB users from https://software.berkeley.edu/mathematica Previous licenses for EECS expired November 30,

Previous licenses for EECS expired November 30, 2014. If you have been using an older version of Matlab with that license server, you can update your licensing configuration to point to the new campus license server See https://iris.eecs.b

ew campus license server See https://iris.eecs.berkeley.edu/15-faq/15-software/mathematica.html for details. MATHEMATICA open-source alternatives ------------------------------------ Open-source alternatives include: Sage (http://www.sagem
ce alternatives include: Sage (http://www.sagemath.org/) Mathics (http://www.mathics.org/) EECS Instructional Support 378/384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
